description_long: This object consists of two prominent pink flowers attached to a woody stem, accompanied by several green leaves. The flowers have broad, overlapping petals with a slightly ruffled edge and a central stamen cluster, typical of azalea or rhododendron species. The petals display a gradient of pink hues, with darker pink veins radiating from the center. The leaves are ovate, medium green, and arranged alternately along the stem, with some showing slight curling or folding. The stems are slender, brown, and woody, supporting both the flowers and leaves. The overall appearance is delicate and vibrant, making it a common ornamental plant in urban parks and gardens.
description: A pair of pink, ruffled flowers with green ovate leaves on a woody stem, displaying vibrant color and delicate structure.
description_view_0: Two pink flowers with ruffled petals and green leaves are seen from the front, showing the full face of the blooms.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the length of the stem and the profile of one flower, with leaves and petals extending outward.
description_view_2: Rear view shows the backs of the flowers and the arrangement of green leaves, with petals less visible.
description_view_3: Top-down view emphasizes the spread of the leaves and the orientation of the flowers along the stem.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, overlapping slightly at the base and forming a symmetrical, whorled pattern typical of many ornamental flowers.
